task_completion.description: Agent logs into the Drupal Commerce administration panel.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Drupal admin toolbar is visible at the top of the screen, indicating a successful login.

task_completion.description: Agent navigates to the Add Product Attribute page.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The browser displays the 'Add product attribute' form in Drupal Commerce.

task_completion.description: Agent sets the attribute label to 'Color'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The word 'Color' is entered into the 'Label' text field on the product attribute form.

task_completion.description: Agent enables the attribute on the 'Default' product variation type.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The checkbox next to the 'Default' product variation type is checked in the attribute configuration form.

task_completion.description: Agent successfully saves the new 'Color' attribute.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A success message indicating the attribute was created is visible, or 'Color' appears in the list on the Product Attributes overview page.

task_completion.description: Agent adds the five specified color values to the attribute.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The attribute values list for 'Color' displays exactly these five values: Midnight Black, Arctic White, Ocean Blue, Forest Green, and Sunset Red.

integrity.description: Agent used the web GUI to configure the attribute and values.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent navigating and filling out forms in the web browser, rather than using terminal commands.

integrity.description: Agent did not use terminal scripts or database commands to bypass the UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal windows should not show 'drush' commands or SQL queries being executed to insert the attribute or its values.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual Drupal Commerce application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser address bar shows 'localhost' and the interface is clearly Drupal Commerce, not a mock-up or text editor.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires creating a Drupal Commerce product attribute labeled 'Color'. It must be enabled on the 'Default' product variation type (machine name: 'default'). Exactly five values must be added to this attribute: Midnight Black, Arctic White, Ocean Blue, Forest Green, and Sunset Red.
